meta.title: Content Collections - Architecture
meta.excerpt: How content collections are structured for articles, tutorials, and case studies in Strapi.

seo.metaTitle: Content Collections in Strapi | CMS Reference
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to model and structure content collections for scalable CMS architecture.

props.text: Content collections define structured, repeatable content entities for your CMS-backed frontend application.

props.title: Best Practice
props.body: Keep collection schemas focused and avoid overloading single entities with too many responsibilities.

props.text: Article collections are the primary content type for blog posts, tutorials, and case studies. Each article has a comprehensive schema with metadata, SEO fields, and dynamic content blocks.

features.title: Taxonomy
features.description: Category relations, tags array, and audience targeting

features.title: SEO Fields
features.description: metaTitle, metaDescription, canonicalUrl, and social preview

features.title: Dynamic Blocks
features.description: Flexible content composition via dynamic zones

props.title: Article Collection Type Definition

props.text: Dynamic zones provide flexible content composition by allowing editors to add, remove, and reorder content blocks. This enables rich, structured content without rigid templates.

props.body:
block.paragraph - Text content with markdown support
• block.sectionHeader - Navigable section markers
• block.codeBlock - Syntax-highlighted code samples
• block.callout - Info/warning/tip/success boxes
• block.list - Ordered or unordered lists
• block.featureGrid - Icon-based feature displays
• block.statsTable - Data tables and metrics
• block.card - Content cards with optional links
• block.collapsible - Expandable content sections
• block.linkCard - Navigation and cross-reference cards

props.title: Content Flexibility
props.body: Dynamic zones enable content creators to compose rich articles without developer intervention for layout changes.

props.text: Shared components are reusable building blocks defined once and used across multiple collection types. This ensures consistency and reduces duplication in content schemas.

links.title: Hero Component
links.description: Standard hero section with title, subtitle, background image, and CTA buttons

links.title: Feature Card
links.description: Icon, title, description pattern used in feature grids

links.title: Testimonial
links.description: Quote, author, company, and optional avatar image

props.title: Component Reusability
props.body: Shared components defined in Strapi can be used in multiple collection types, ensuring consistent structure and reducing maintenance overhead.

props.text: Content collections are organized hierarchically with categories, subcategories, and tags to enable flexible navigation and content discovery.

features.title: Tags
features.description: Cross-cutting topics for content filtering and related content discovery

features.title: Relationships
features.description: Explicit content relationships for navigation and recommendations

props.body:
Category slugs map directly to URL structure
• Tags enable cross-category content discovery
• Audience field helps personalize documentation views
• Related content is auto-suggested based on category and tags

props.title: Best Practice
props.body: Use consistent category slugs and tag patterns across all content collections to enable effective search and navigation.
